where_to_eat.name: Roscioli
where_to_eat.description: Praised for excellent pizza in Rome. A local favorite that offers quality dining experience.

where_to_eat.name: Osteria del Tempo Perso Belsiana
where_to_eat.description: Located at Via Belsiana, 94-95. Known for traditional Italian cuisine, though tiramisu received lower ratings in taste comparisons.

gram_worthy_spots.name: The Pantheon
gram_worthy_spots.description: An iconic Roman structure with a famous oculus (opening) in the roof that creates a magical experience, especially when it rains. A must-see photogenic landmark.

gram_worthy_spots.name: St. Peter's Basilica
gram_worthy_spots.description: Home to Michelangelo's Pieta sculpture, one of the most stunning artworks in the world. A breathtaking and visually memorable location.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Villa D'Este
off_the_beaten_path.description: Located in nearby Tivoli (a quick drive from Rome), this villa features grandiose gardens and is a pleasant surprise for those seeking architecture and nature away from the city center.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Villa Gregoriana
off_the_beaten_path.description: Also in Tivoli, this estate offers winding paths, caves, and waterfalls set into the surrounding valley's geography, providing a peaceful escape from Rome's tourist crowds.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Tivoli
off_the_beaten_path.description: A charming town just outside Rome featuring understated architecture that incorporates the surrounding valley's geography, offering a less crowded alternative to the main city.
